# scalegrid Provider

The ScaleGrid provider manages database deployments (MongoDB, Redis, MySQL, PostgreSQL) and related resources through the ScaleGrid console API.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    scalegrid = {
      source  = "requestflo/scalegrid"
      version = "~> 0.1"
    }
  }
}

provider "scalegrid" {
  # Credentials can also be supplied via SCALEGRID_EMAIL and SCALEGRID_PASSWORD.
  # The provider authenticates against the ScaleGrid console (console.scalegrid.io)
  # using a session cookie. For automation, use an account with 2FA disabled.
  email    = "you@example.com"
  password = var.scalegrid_password
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `base_url` (String) Base URL of the ScaleGrid console. Defaults to `https://console.scalegrid.io`. May also be set with `SCALEGRID_BASE_URL`. For a dedicated/on-prem controller, set this to your controller's URL.
- `email` (String) ScaleGrid account email. May also be set with `SCALEGRID_EMAIL`.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) ScaleGrid account password. May also be set with `SCALEGRID_PASSWORD`.
- `two_factor_code` (String, Sensitive) Optional two-factor (TOTP) code. Because TOTP codes expire within seconds, this is only practical for one-shot runs; for automation, use an account with 2FA disabled. May also be set with `SCALEGRID_TWO_FACTOR_CODE`.
